market_session: regular US cash-equity session, third session after Juneteenth closure
text: The June 24, 2026 report date was a regular Wednesday U.S. cash-equity session, the third session after the Juneteenth and weekend break, so sentiment values can be read against a live U.S. closing tape.

value.context: Mixed close: more stocks rose than fell within the S&P 500 despite index decline; tech giants including Microsoft weighed; Brent crude dropped 3.8%; Treasury yields fell; rotation into cyclicals away from mega-cap tech

text: AP reported a mixed June 24 U.S. close: the S&P 500 fell 7.24 points or 0.1% to 7,358.22 and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 110.40 points or 0.4% to 25,476.64 as losses for tech giants including Microsoft weighed on the market. The Dow rose 182.06 points or 0.4% to 51,848.90 and the Russell 2000 gained 11.15 points or 0.4% to 2,986.63. AP noted that more stocks rose than fell within the S&P 500 despite the index decline, signaling internal breadth improvement even as the headline stayed negative.

subject.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
subject.source_basis: CNN Fear & Greed page corroborated by Finhacker.cz CNN-tracker

value.methodology_note: CNN Stock Price Breadth indicator still reflects the McClellan Summation Index methodology change noted in prior reports
value.freshness_note: Neither publisher had posted a verified June 24 post-close update at collection time; CNN 27 reflects an early-June-24 pre-market snapshot while Finhacker 28 is the June 23 post-close value

market_session: latest available readings at collection time
text: The Fear & Greed Index remained in fear territory on June 24. CNN's page displayed 27 (fear) with 'Previous close 27', '1 week ago 32', '1 month ago 58', and 'Last updated Jun 24 at 5:18:16 AM ET'. Finhacker.cz's CNN-tracker still showed 28 (fear) with a data-updated timestamp of 'Jun 23, 2026, 8:00 PM ET'. Neither source carried a verified June 24 post-close update at collection time, so the latest available reading is the CNN 27 value, which is one point below the Finhacker June 23 post-close value of 28.

market_session: Cboe published volatility-index data for the regular US session

calculation.method: VIX one-day percent change = (18.63 - 19.49) / 19.49 * 100 = -4.41%; curve compares Cboe close fields for VIX9D, VIX, VIX3M, and VIX6M on 2026-06-24

text: Yahoo Finance historical data showed VIX closing at 18.63 on June 24, down 0.86 points or 4.41% from 19.49 on June 23, with OHLC of open 19.13, high 20.34, low 18.04, close 18.63. The same-date Cboe volatility curve was upward sloping with a restored normal front: VIX9D at 18.07, VIX at 18.63, VIX3M at 20.37, and VIX6M at 22.40. The VIX9D-to-VIX gap widened to 0.56 points (VIX9D below VIX), restoring the typical upward slope after June 23's near-flat 0.02-point spread. The VIX9D intraday range of 17.00 to 20.67 remained wide, reflecting two-way volatility during the session before the vol-easing close.

market_session: latest AAII weekly survey available for the June 24 report date

calculation.method: Bull-bear spread = bullish 36.6% minus bearish 39.4% = -2.8 percentage points

text: The latest AAII sentiment survey (week ending 6/17/2026, published June 18) showed bullish sentiment at 36.6%, neutral at 24.1%, and bearish at 39.4%, leaving a bull-bear spread of -2.8 percentage points. This is the same reading available for the June 23 report and does not reflect the June 23-24 price action. The next AAII release is expected Thursday June 25.

value.data_freshness_note: No new weekly fund-flow releases verified on the live ICI or ETF.com pages between the June 23 and June 24 report dates; both readings predate the June 23-24 risk-off action

market_session: latest fund-flow releases available for the June 24 report date
text: No new verified weekly fund-flow data was available for the June 24 report date. ICI's latest combined-flow release (published June 17 for the week ended June 10) showed $31.52 billion of total long-term inflows, including $48.33 billion of ETF net issuance, $16.64 billion into equity funds, $18.21 billion into bond funds, and $2.27 billion out of commodity funds. ETF.com's June 22 weekly article reported $119.83 billion of total ETF inflows led by $83.62 billion into U.S. equity ETFs. These readings provide positioning context heading into the June 23-24 action, not a real-time flow gauge.

value.treasury_note: Treasury yields fell in the bond market
value.macro_theme: Iran de-escalation, dollar strength, rate-hike pricing, falling oil

text: Reuters reported Brent crude settled at $73.74, down 4.33% on the day and nearing pre-Iran-war levels as Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic improved. The dollar index rose 0.24% to 101.63, its highest since May 2025, as markets priced in at least one Fed rate hike. Gold fell 3.2% to $3,976.73 on the stronger dollar. Treasury yields fell in the bond market. The macro backdrop drove a rotation into cyclicals, industrials, materials, and consumer discretionary, and out of tech and energy.

value.cash_equity_tape: mixed close with S&P -0.1%, Nasdaq -0.4%, Dow +0.4%, Russell +0.4%; more stocks rose than fell within S&P despite index decline
value.survey_sentiment: net bearish, unchanged from prior survey, does not reflect June 23-24 action
value.volatility: VIX -4.41% to 18.63; upward-sloping curve restored with VIX9D back below VIX
value.flows: latest available flows are pre-selloff; still showing strong equity allocation
value.risk_read: risk-off intensity faded from June 23, but sentiment remains in fear zone and tech/AI overhang persists into Micron earnings

market_session: regular US cash-equity session synthesis from latest available sentiment indicators
text: The best synthesis for June 24 is 'fragile stabilization, tech still bleeding.' June 23's unambiguous risk-off broke on June 24 into a mixed close: the Dow and Russell 2000 each rose 0.4%, AP reported more advancers than decliners within the S&P 500, and the VIX eased 4.41% to 18.63 with the VIX9D-to-VIX spread restoring a normal upward slope. But the S&P 500 still fell 0.1% and the Nasdaq dropped 0.4% as tech giants including Microsoft continued to weigh ahead of Micron earnings. The Fear & Greed Index stayed in fear at 27-28, the AAII survey is unchanged and doesn't reflect the two-day action, and the latest flow readings still predate the selloff. The regime is therefore less risk-off than June 23 but not risk-on — it is a fragile, rotation-driven stabilization with an unresolved tech/AI overhang.
